    Trade Explained
    We paid $2.82 betting that Dallas's high temperature will settle outside 88–89°F.
    Why We Think We're Right
    GFS (83°F) and ECMWF (85°F) agree — 30 GFS ensemble members average 94°F (29% GFS / 52% ECMWF). HRRR (3km local model) shows 83°F. Blended forecast: 85°F. Market prices our winning scenario at 67%; we calculate 90% — 23% mispriced. Live temp is 93°F (4.2°F cushion).
    What Could Go Wrong
    If the actual high temperature misses our 85°F forecast by more than 3°F in the wrong direction, we lose $2.82.

    We paid $0.77 buying NO on Washington Dc's daily HIGH landing in 65–66°F. Forecast is 0°F outside the bracket. Win $0.25/contract if correct.
    Why We Think We're Right
    [daily HIGH] 4-model blend forecasts 61°F — 0.0°F outside this bracket. GFS 69°F and ECMWF 57°F diverge — both confirm forecast is clear of bracket. P(NOT in bracket) = 97%. Market YES at 75% is overpriced → NO at 25% has 22% edge. Risk/reward: risk 75% to win 25% (0.3:1).
    What Could Go Wrong
    A 0°F+ forecast error would push the actual into this bracket. Sigma is 2.1°F — unlikely but not impossible. We lose $0.77.

    Why We Think We're Right
    [daily HIGH] 4-model blend forecasts 53°F — 0.0°F outside this bracket. GFS 49°F and ECMWF 56°F diverge — both confirm forecast is clear of bracket. P(NOT in bracket) = 97%. Market YES at 76% is overpriced → NO at 24% has 21% edge. Risk/reward: risk 76% to win 24% (0.3:1).

    A 0°F+ forecast error would push the actual into this bracket. Sigma is 2.2°F — unlikely but not impossible. We lose $0.78.

    We paid $0.72 betting that Philadelphia's high temperature will settle outside 72–73°F.
    Why We Think We're Right
    GFS (69°F) and ECMWF (67°F) are close — 30 GFS ensemble members average 67°F (18% GFS / 35% ECMWF). HRRR (3km local model) shows 69°F. Blended forecast: 69°F. Market prices our winning scenario at 71%; we calculate 97% — 26% mispriced. Live temp is 73°F — only 0.4°F from the boundary.

Deep dive into each bot's performance, settings, and learning history
🌤
Weather (HIGH)
scans markets → calculates edge → trades
$+0.00
P&L
$+11.73

Record 0W 0L 16P Allocation $300 Max Trades 99
🌤 How This Bot Works tap to expand
Forecast Accuracy Edge (HIGH)
Compares 4 weather models (GFS, ECMWF, HRRR, ICON) against Kalshi market prices for daily high temperatures in 20 cities. When our models strongly disagree with the market, we trade.
Settlement NWS Daily Climate Report (official government measurement) Best At Clear-sky days where models agree strongly Worst At Marine layer (LA), lake effect (Chicago), frontal boundaries
Settings
Min. Advantage 0% Max Lookahead 168h Portfolio Share 30% Related Group physical
Tuned Values
ScopeParamValueSource
atlanta bias_correction 0.00 default
atlanta blend_sigma_all 1.84 calibration_refresh
atlanta blend_sigma_fall 1.66 calibration_refresh
atlanta blend_sigma_spring 1.89 calibration_refresh
atlanta blend_sigma_summer 2.02 calibration_refresh
atlanta blend_sigma_winter 1.89 calibration_refresh
atlanta city_confidence_multiplier 1.00 reset_neutral
atlanta edge_threshold 0.15 calibration_2026_04_09
atlanta sigma_multiplier 1.30 calibration_2026_04_09

❄ How This Bot Works tap to expand
Overnight Low Edge (LOW) — Rebuilt
4-model blend (GFS+ECMWF+HRRR+ICON) with regime-based per-city weights. ECMWF-dominant (45–65%). Physics overlays: radiative cooling, dewpoint floor, frontal passage, precipitation/snow. −2°F warm-bias correction. Evening boost 1.15× after 18:00 local.
Settlement NWS Daily Climate Report — official overnight minimum Best At Evening entries (18:00+ local), clear/calm continental_radcool cities, 20%+ edge Worst At Morning entries (0.70× penalty pre-noon), frontal-passage nights, marine intrusions
Settings
Min. Advantage 8% Max Lookahead 72h Portfolio Share 5% Related Group physical
Model Weights & Calibration (LOW)
Per-city 4-model regime-based weights · σ multiplier · backfill MAE
CityTierσ× GFS ECMWF HRRR ICON MAERegime

🌧 How This Bot Works tap to expand
Precipitation Probability Edge
3-model blend (GFS 20%, ECMWF 40%, HRRR 40%) for daily P(rain ≥ 0.01 in). Binary YES/NO markets per city. NWS METAR live override: if already raining, P(rain) floors at 0.95. Eighth-Kelly sizing.
Settlement NWS Daily Climate Report (precipitation ≥ 0.01 in = YES) Best At Clear-cut dry days or active rain events (high model agreement) Worst At Marginal drizzle/trace precipitation near 0.01 in threshold
Settings
Min. Advantage 10% Max Lookahead 48h Portfolio Share 5% Related Group physical
🎯
Base Rate NO
scans markets → calculates edge → trades
$+0.00
P&L
$-0.20
Unrealized

Exposed
Record 0W 0L 4P Allocation $50 Max Trades 5
🎯 How This Bot Works tap to expand
Base Rate NO — Overpriced Bracket Fader
Buys NO on narrow 2°F temperature brackets priced YES $0.04–$0.14 where our 4-model forecast is 4+°F away. Targets structurally overpriced brackets via favorite-longshot bias. Uses same forecast data as HIGH/LOW bots — no extra API calls. ~85–95% win rate, profit from volume across 20 cities.
Settlement NWS Daily Climate Report (same as Weather HIGH/LOW) Best At Distant brackets where forecast strongly disagrees with market pricing Worst At Large forecast errors (4+°F miss) that push actual temp into a distant bracket
Settings
Min. Advantage 3% Max Lookahead 168h Portfolio Share 5% Related Group physical

Trading Mode
Current operational state
Mode PAPER — simulated trades Testing Mode ON — capped at 40% exposure / 4% per trade Balance Source Kalshi API Kalshi Cash (real) $383.47 Kalshi Total $383.47 Paper Bankroll $1,000.00 Kalshi Positions 0
Fleet Rules
Tiered limits — tightens automatically as portfolio grows
Live Balance $1,000.00 Starting Bankroll $1,000 Risk Tier testing Max at Risk 40% = $400 Max per Trade 4.0% = $40.00 Max Open Trades 99 Max per Cycle 5 trades / 15min Min Net Expected Profit 0.020% = $0.20 (after fees) Min Score Threshold 0.35
Deployment Tiers — auto-selects based on bankroll
Tier Max Exposure Per Trade Example $ Why
▸ Testing40%4.0%$40.00Validate with real money before scaling
$0–$1K40%5.0%$50 on $1KSmall bankroll — aggressive growth
$1K–$10K30%3.0%$150 on $5KProven edge — steady compounding
$10K–$50K20%2.0%$500 on $25KProtect capital — liquidity matters
$50K+15%1.5%$750 on $50KPreserve wealth — max caution
How it works
• Max Exposure — total $ at risk across ALL open trades combined
• Per Trade — largest single bet allowed (Kelly sizing picks the actual amount within this cap)
• Higher probability + higher edge = bigger bet (Kelly Criterion), but never exceeds per-trade cap
• Tiers tighten automatically as balance grows — bigger bankrolls need more protection
• Unfilled orders (thin liquidity) cost nothing — you only risk what actually fills
Risk Protection
Two-tier safety — rolling window since trades settle in hours/days
🟡
Cooldown
Lost 4.0% of bankroll over 7 days → slow to 2 trade/cycle ($1K–$10K tier)
$40.00
🛑
Max Drawdown
Lost 15% from all-time peak → FULL STOP, manual review
$150.00
All Trade Gates (10 checks before any trade)
Every signal must pass ALL of these before real money is risked
1
Minimum Score
Signal score must be ≥ 0.35 (combines edge, probability, time, liquidity, diversity). Below this = noise, not signal.
2
Max Entry Price
Entry price must be ≤ $0.55. Above that, risk/reward is skewed — you're risking $0.55+ to win $0.45. Not worth it.
3
No Duplicate Markets
Can't bet on the same ticker twice. If you already hold NYC high above 54°F, another bet on the same market is blocked.
4
Smart Bracket Stacking + Hedge Analysis
Max 2 brackets per event. Same-direction stacks: 30% Kelly penalty. Conflicting-direction hedges: allowed if edge ≥ 40% AND prob ≥ 55%, with 60% Kelly penalty. Max 10% of bankroll per event.
5
Max Open Trades
Fleet-wide cap of 99 open trades. Prevents over-diversification where you can't monitor what you own.
6
Minimum Net Expected Profit
Trade's expected profit must be ≥ 0.020% of bankroll ($0.20) AFTER subtracting Kalshi's entry fee. Set low so high-conviction longshots with small but real edge can still trade — the gate only blocks cases where fees eat the edge entirely.
7
Max Same Bot Per Cycle
Max 5 trades from the same bot per 30-min scan. Prevents one bot from dominating every cycle.
8
Per-Bot Trade Cap
Per-bot caps lifted (99 each) — capital allocation via Kelly + exposure tiers is now the binding constraint, not trade counts.
9
Correlation Group Limit
Correlated bots (Weather + Energy share "physical" group) can't exceed 50% of bankroll combined. If heat drives both, one bad forecast loses on both.
10
Total Exposure Limit
All open trades combined can't exceed 40% of bankroll ($400.00). The rest stays in cash as a safety buffer.
Exit Rules (checked every scan on open positions)
Edges are recalculated each 15-min cycle — if a thesis breaks, we sell before settlement
TAKE_PROFIT
Price ≥ 3× entry AND net P&L positive → lock in the win. (Was 5×; lowered so we capture more realized gains.)
TRAILING_STOP
Peak ≥ 3× entry then price falls ≥ 50% from peak → exit to protect giveback.
EDGE_FLIP
Recomputed edge flipped sign — model now disagrees with the direction we bought. Cut immediately.
EDGE_DECAY
Remaining edge collapsed to near zero — the market caught up to our thesis. Free the capital.
STOP_LOSS
Price dropped ≥ 30% from entry AND model confidence dropped ≥ 10% — BOTH required so noise alone won't trigger it.
TIME_STOP
Within 3h of settlement, underwater, and current probability < 45% → take the small loss rather than ride to zero.
Forecast Cache & Data Sources
Disk-cached forecasts aligned to model runs — scan Kalshi prices every 15 min on ~120 API calls/day
Cache TTL: 6 hours (aligned to GFS/ECMWF 00/06/12/18 UTC runs). HRRR uses 1.5h since it updates hourly.
Primary source: Open-Meteo (free gateway to GFS, ECMWF, HRRR, GEFS ensemble).
Fallback: NWS api.weather.gov — auto-engages after 3 consecutive 429s from Open-Meteo. Single-source, so sigma widened 15% for confidence penalty.
Reconciliation: Auto-fixes ghosts (marks settled), orphans (creates local record), and mismatches (trusts Kalshi as truth) — no manual intervention needed.
Forecast Accuracy (TRACK 1) — last 14 days
Raw forecast vs NWS actual, T+1 horizon. Drives per-city bias correction independent of trade outcomes.
CityNMean errMAE

Edge Calibration (TRACK 2)
Claimed edge vs realized win rate, bucketed. Surfaces overconfidence without changing the model.
No calibration runs yet — TRACK 2 runs every Sunday 7 PM ET. Needs ≥20 settled trades per city.

Per-Bot Settings
Each bot scans with full bankroll — best trades win regardless of source
🌤 Weather (HIGH) 🟢
Min Edge 0% Max Open 99 trades Max Lookahead 168h (7d) Corr. Group physical
❄ Weather (LOW) 🔴
Min Edge 8% Max Open 5 trades Max Lookahead 72h (3d) Corr. Group physical
🌧 Rain 🔴
Min Edge 10% Max Open 5 trades Max Lookahead 48h (2d) Corr. Group physical
🎯 Base Rate NO 🟢
Min Edge 3% Max Open 5 trades Max Lookahead 168h (7d) Corr. Group physical
🌤️ Weather Bot — Complete Rule Set
Every rule, threshold, and calibration value the weather bot uses to decide trades
📊 Edge & Conviction Thresholds
A trade must pass ALL of these to be considered. If any one fails, the trade is skipped.
Min Edge (global) 20% Min Conviction 60% Max Forecast Horizon 168h (7 days) Min Volume $0 (waived for weather)
Edge = how much our probability differs from Kalshi's price. 20% means we think a bet is worth ≥20¢ more than the market says.
Conviction = our estimated probability of winning. 60% means we only bet when we think there's a 60%+ chance we're right.
Backtest insight: All losses came from coin-flip bets (42% conviction). Wins clustered at 79%+ conviction.
💰 Position Sizing (Kelly Criterion)
Kelly tells you the mathematically optimal bet size. We use quarter-Kelly for safety — slower growth but much less risk of ruin.
Kelly Cap 8% of bankroll Kelly Multiplier 12.5% (eighth-Kelly) Max Exposure 50% of bankroll Max Drawdown 25% from peak
Formula: kelly_f = (p_win × payout - p_lose) / payout, then × 0.25, capped at 15%
Example: If win prob = 75%, payout = 2:1 → Kelly says bet 37.5% → quarter-Kelly = 9.4% → capped at 9.4% of bankroll
Drawdown halt: If balance drops 25% from its peak, all trading stops automatically until you manually review.
🏙️ Per-City Calibration
Not all cities are equally easy to forecast. Harder cities get wider uncertainty (sigma) and higher edge requirements.
City Station Tier GFS w ECMWF w HRRR w ICON w σ × Bias °F Min Edge 2yr MAE
🍑 Atlanta KATL A 0.22 0.32 0.21 0.26 2.00× 0.0 15% 1.64°
🤠 Austin KAUS A 0.21 0.33 0.21 0.26 2.00× 0.0 15% 1.61°
🦞 Boston KBOS A 0.16 0.44 0.17 0.24 2.00× 0.0 15% 1.47°
🌬️ Chicago KMDW A 0.18 0.36 0.22 0.23 2.00× +1.1 15% 1.45°
🤠 Dallas KDFW B 0.23 0.32 0.20 0.26 2.00× 0.0 15% 1.87°
🏔️ Denver KDEN A 0.22 0.32 0.22 0.24 2.00× -2.0 15% 1.57°
🛢️ Houston KHOU B 0.23 0.32 0.23 0.23 1.15× 0.0 15% 1.87°
🎰 Las Vegas KLAS A 0.19 0.38 0.25 0.18 1.15× 0.0 15% 1.10°
🌴 Los Angeles KLAX D 0.32 0.10 0.33 0.25 2.00× -2.0 15% 2.65°
🌊 Miami KMIA A 0.25 0.26 0.25 0.25 2.00× +0.7 15% 1.51°
❄️ Minneapolis KMSP A 0.19 0.38 0.19 0.25 1.15× +1.9 15% 1.55°
🎷 New Orleans KMSY A 0.20 0.38 0.20 0.22 1.15× 0.0 15% 1.64°
🗽 New York City KNYC A 0.20 0.37 0.20 0.22 1.00× +0.4 15% 1.61°
🌪️ Oklahoma City KOKC A 0.21 0.33 0.21 0.26 1.15× -0.3 15% 1.71°
🔔 Philadelphia KPHL A 0.23 0.32 0.21 0.25 1.44× +1.7 15% 1.63°
🌵 Phoenix KPHX A 0.22 0.34 0.20 0.25 1.44× +0.9 15% 1.01°
🌮 San Antonio KSAT B 0.18 0.38 0.18 0.25 1.15× +1.6 15% 1.81°
🌉 San Francisco KSFO D 0.35 0.25 0.25 0.15 1.15× +2.0 15% 2.76°
☔ Seattle KSEA A 0.22 0.29 0.22 0.26 1.15× +0.5 15% 1.65°
🏛️ Washington Dc KDCA B 0.19 0.36 0.18 0.26 1.15× 0.0 15% 1.83°
σ Multiplier widens the uncertainty bell curve — 1.50× means we treat Miami forecasts as 50% less reliable than NYC.
Bias shifts the forecast before calculating probability. LA -2°F means models tend to over-predict LA temps by 2 degrees.
Min Edge is the per-city override — LA needs 12% edge (vs 8% for NYC) because forecasts are less reliable there.
🌡️ Forecast Uncertainty Schedule (σ by Hours Out)
How much uncertainty (in °F) we add based on how far out the forecast is. Closer = more accurate = tighter bell curve.
6h
±3.0°F
12h
±3.5°F
24h (1d)
±4.5°F
48h (2d)
±5.5°F
72h (3d)
±6.0°F
96h (4d)
±6.5°F
120h (5d)
±7.5°F
168h (7d)
±8.5°F
Based on NWS/ECMWF verification data. A 6-hour forecast is accurate within ±2°F. A 7-day forecast may be off by ±7.5°F.
City sigma multipliers are applied on top of these base values (e.g., Miami 7-day: 7.5 × 1.5 = ±11.3°F).
🧠 Weather Model Ensemble
We don't trust any single weather model. Three models vote, and we weight their probabilities.
HRRR
40%
NOAA's high-res rapid refresh.
Best for same-day (0-18h).
Only available for US cities.
GFS
40%
Global Forecast System.
Solid 1-7 day forecasts.
Updates every 6 hours.
ECMWF
20%
European model (IFS).
Best for 3-7 day range.
Lower weight to avoid over-reliance.
When HRRR is unavailable (international cities or forecast too far out): GFS gets 65%, ECMWF gets 35%.
Consensus bonus: When all models agree (probabilities within 5%), conviction gets a boost — stronger signal.
🚫 Bracket Dead Zone Rules
We avoid betting on brackets where the forecast lands right on the boundary — too risky, like flipping a coin.
⛔ If forecast is within 1σ of a bracket boundary → skip (probability too uncertain)
⚠️ If forecast is within 2σ → edge must be extra large to compensate for boundary risk
✅ If forecast is >2σ from boundaries → standard rules apply, trade normally
Why? If a bracket is "High ≥ 70°F" and our forecast is 70.5°F with σ = 2°F, a slight model error flips the outcome.
Dead zones protect us from the trades where being right or wrong is basically random.
⚖️ Hedge Trade Analysis
When a new signal conflicts directionally with an existing bet in the same event, the bot runs a risk analysis instead of an automatic block.
⚖️ Hedge min edge: 40% (2× the normal threshold — conflicting bets need much stronger conviction)
⚖️ Hedge min probability: 55% (must be confident the new signal will win)
⚖️ Kelly penalty: 60% reduction (bet size cut by 60% vs normal)
✅ Both-win zone: When brackets overlap (e.g., NO on B51.5 + YES on B53.5), there's a temperature range where both bets profit simultaneously
Why allow hedges? Weather conditions change. If models now strongly disagree with our original bet direction (64% edge!), the new data likely has information our original bet didn't have. Blocking a high-conviction signal just because we have an existing bet means leaving money on the table.
Protection: The 40% edge + 55% probability minimums ensure we only hedge when the new signal is extremely strong — not on marginal calls.
🔄 Trade Execution Rules
📝 Order type: Limit orders only — never market orders. We set our price, wait for fill.
⏱️ Fill timeout: 30 seconds — if nobody takes our limit order, it's cancelled automatically.
📊 Partial fills: If only some contracts fill, we keep what filled and cancel the rest.
💲 Side selection: Always pick the side (YES/NO) with the higher expected payout — never biased toward YES or NO.
🎯 Entry cap: Never pay more than 85¢ per contract — high prices leave no room for profit after fees.
🔁 Scan interval: Every 15 minutes — checks for new markets, settles completed trades, re-evaluates positions.

Calibration Error = avg |stated prob − win rate| across probability bands. Lower is better.
EIA Surprise Win Rate vs No EIA Win Rate: if EIA >> baseline, the surprise signal is adding real edge.
Multi-Signal = trades where 2+ collector signals fired simultaneously.
🌙 LOW Temperature Bot — Calibration
Daily LOW bracket markets (KXLOWT*). Fully isolated from the HIGH pipeline: separate scopes ({city}_low), separate skip log, separate settlement path.
Status: PAUSED — rebuilt with new calibration, awaiting activation
4-model blend · σ 1.5–2.5× · −2°F bias · 20% edge threshold · evening boost 1.15×
Physics Overlays
☁ Cloud/wind rad-cool
💧 Dewpoint floor
🌬 Frontal passage
🌧 Precip / snow cover
Timing Preference
▼ Pre-noon 0.70× edge (−30%)
● Noon–18:00 1.00× (neutral)
▲ After 18:00 1.15× edge (+15%)

🎷 New Orleans D 1.80× 20% 10% 55% 10% 25% marine 1.00°
🗽 New York City D 1.80× 20% 8% 65% 5% 22% continental_radcool 1.11°
🌪️ Oklahoma City E 2.00× 20% 10% 55% 12% 23% continental 0.99°
🔔 Philadelphia D 1.80× 20% 8% 65% 5% 22% continental_radcool 1.25°
🌵 Phoenix F 2.50× 20% 15% 50% 15% 20% desert_dry 2.06°
🌮 San Antonio E 2.00× 20% 10% 60% 8% 22% subtropical 1.13°
🌉 San Francisco D 2.00× 20% 10% 55% 10% 25% marine 1.02°
☔ Seattle D 2.00× 20% 10% 55% 10% 25% marine 0.93°
🏛️ Washington Dc D 1.80× 20% 8% 65% 5% 22% continental_radcool 1.41°
ECMWF dominates all regimes (45–65%) — wins 18/20 cities on overnight lows in 2yr backfill.
HRRR gets the highest weight in Denver (20%) where 3km terrain resolution matters for valley cold pools. Continental cities (OKC 12%) get moderate HRRR weight; HRRR warm-biased on lows in urban/radcool cities (5%).
ICON (25%) leads in marine cities — European physics core handles marine boundary layers well.
Bias −2°F seeded from warm-bias prior; learning.db refines this live. ERA5 quarantine rule: backfill sets prior only.
📊 LOW Bot — Learning Status
Per-settlement accuracy from live NWS CLI readings vs model forecasts. Populated after trades settle — 0 obs is normal until first LOW trade resolves. bias_correction is ERA5-quarantined (locked until TRACK 1 accumulates live data).
City Obs Blend MAE Blend Bias Physics Δ Best Model GFS MAE ECMWF MAE Bias Lock
Austin 1 0.95° +0.95° — ECMWF 2.30° 0.60° ERA5 🔒
Las Vegas 1 6.45° +6.45° — ICON 5.50° 4.20° ERA5 🔒
Physics Δ = Blend MAE − Pre-physics MAE. Negative = physics overlays improved accuracy. Positive = physics hurt.
Bias Lock = ERA5 🔒 means bias_correction is quarantined (need ≥5 live obs). "Live (N)" means unlocked.
🌧 Rain Bot — Status (NYC only)
Binary YES/NO markets (KXRAINNYC). YES wins if NWS CLI records ≥0.01 in of precipitation. 3-model blend (GFS 20% · ECMWF 40% · HRRR 40%). Separate skip log: data/rain_skip_log.json.
Status: PAUSED — activate via POST /api/kill-switch/resume/rain
Eighth-Kelly · 20-contract cap · $5 max · 10% edge threshold · 60% model agreement required
Data Sources
GFS seamless (20%)
ECMWF IFS (40%)
HRRR 3km (40%)
HRRR: today+tomorrow only
Signal Logic
Max hourly P(precip) → day prob
NWS live obs: already raining → 0.95 floor
Agreement boost if all >90%
Already-raining boost: ×1.20 edge
Settlement Source
NWS CLI Daily Report
Station: KNYC (Central Park)
Same product as HIGH/LOW bots
Trace (T) = 0.00 in → NO win
Skip Reasons
insufficient_edge (<10%)
low_model_agreement (<60%)
insufficient_volume (<$50)
single_model_only
kill_switch_active

🎯 Base Rate NO — Strategy & Status
Buys NO on narrow 2°F temperature brackets where YES is priced $0.04–$0.14 and forecast is 4+°F away from the bracket. Exploits favorite-longshot bias across all 20 cities (HIGH + LOW markets). ~85–95% win rate.
Status: RUNNING
Eighth-Kelly · 20-contract cap · 2% per trade · 10% total exposure · 3% min edge
Filters (12-gate pipeline)
YES price $0.04–$0.14
Forecast 4+°F from bracket edge
P(NO) ≥ 80%
NO spread ≤ 10¢
Volume ≥ $50 · Kelly > $0.50
Signal Logic
4-model blend (same as HIGH/LOW)
Distance = forecast − nearest bracket edge
P(NO) = 1 − P(temp in bracket)
Always trades NO side only
Settlement Source
NWS CLI Daily Report
Same stations as HIGH/LOW
CLI rounding: bracket 62–63 covers [61.5, 63.5)
Cross-checked against determine_winner()
Skip Reasons
yes_price_too_low (<$0.04)
yes_price_too_high (>$0.14)
forecast_too_close (<4°F)
no_positive_edge
prob_no_too_low (<80%)
